response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine developer-tooling experience, including work on a commercial C IDE (LabWindows/CVI) and authoring test runners and assertion libraries. However, the role's core requirements — deep Java and Kotlin expertise, JVM internals, and solid Spring ecosystem experience — are essentially absent. Java appears only peripherally in past roles, Kotlin and Spring not at all, and recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. The tooling passion fits, but the JVM-stack gap is too large for a mid-senior enterprise Java/Kotlin IDE position.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Demonstrated passion for developer tooling and language tooling (test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, community teaching
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, both required qualifications
Java exposure is minor and dated; no evidence of deep JVM understanding
Recent career trajectory (TypeScript/Node, full-stack, AI tooling) diverges from enterprise JVM tooling focus
